technical-context.architecture-pattern: Ordered fallback chain with circuit breaker, budget checking, and instrumented wrapping
technical-context.primary-goal: Given a list of ProviderChainEntry[], try each in order, skip unhealthy/over-budget providers, wrap the first available provider with InstrumentedAgentProvider, and record all attempts in diagnostics

core-interface.name: IProviderChain
core-interface.description: Interface for the provider chain. Story 9-8's RoleBasedAgentResolver depends on this interface, not the concrete ProviderChain class.

method.name: getProvider
method.signature: getProvider(context: { agentType: AgentType; projectId: string; engineId: string }): Promise<IAgentProvider>

core-class.name: ProviderChain
core-class.description: Iterates an ordered list of ProviderChainEntry[] to find the first healthy, budget-compliant, available provider. Wraps the result with InstrumentedAgentProvider for diagnostics. Records failures into IProviderHealthTracker for circuit breaker logic. Safe for concurrent getProvider() calls -- state management (health, budget) is delegated to injected services. No internal mutable state beyond the frozen entries array. Per-entry retries are not supported; transient failures are handled by the circuit breaker's half-open recovery mechanism and by the caller retrying chain resolution.

constructor-dependencies.dependency: Ordered list of provider+model combos to try. Defensively copied and frozen in constructor.
Creates IAgentProvider instances from ProviderChainEntry (interface from Story 9-4, not concrete AgentProviderFactory)
Circuit breaker: tracks failures, marks providers unhealthy (interface from Story 9-3, not concrete ProviderHealthTracker)
Shared telemetry queue for provider call instrumentation
Budget checking via checkLimit() before attempting a provider

method.name: getProvider
method.signature: async getProvider(context: { agentType: AgentType; projectId: string; engineId: string }): Promise<IAgentProvider>
method.description: Returns the first available healthy provider, wrapped with InstrumentedAgentProvider

key-behaviors.behavior: Iterates entries in priority order (first entry = highest priority)
Guards empty providerChain with explicit EMPTY_PROVIDER_CHAIN error (retryable: false, severity: 'critical') before iterating
Skips circuit-open providers via health.isHealthy(key) -- uses IProviderHealthTracker interface
Checks budget via costTracker.checkLimit() before attempting (with fail-closed policy: if checkLimit() throws, skip the provider and log warning)
Wraps returned provider with InstrumentedAgentProvider for diagnostics/cost tracking
Does NOT call recordSuccess() after isAvailable() -- success recording is InstrumentedAgentProvider's responsibility on actual task completion
Calls provider.dispose() when isAvailable() returns false to prevent resource leaks
Calls provider.dispose() on error; dispose errors are logged (not silently swallowed)
Uses isProviderError() type guard before passing errors to recordFailure() -- no unsafe casts
Throws NO_AVAILABLE_PROVIDER with provider names only in message; detailed error info in structured context (sanitized -- no raw e.message in the error message string)
Unknown provider strings do not crash budget check (safe Set-based validation)
Uses ProviderHealthTracker.buildKey() for centralized key construction (not inline template literal)
Module-level KNOWN_PROVIDERS constant (not re-created per iteration)
Constructor defensively copies and freezes entries array
NO_AVAILABLE_PROVIDER has retryable: false -- the chain itself does not retry. Callers (engine) should retry the entire chain resolution with backoff if all providers are temporarily circuit-open.
Per-entry retries are not supported. Transient failures are handled by the circuit breaker's half-open recovery mechanism and by the caller retrying chain resolution.

provider-key-format.description: Provider health key format: "provider:model" e.g. "openrouter:z-ai/z1-mini", "claude-code:default". Key construction is centralized via ProviderHealthTracker.buildKey().

budget-check.description: Before attempting a provider, check ICostTracker.checkLimit() if costTracker is provided. Wrapped in try/catch with fail-closed policy.
budget-check.known-providers-constant: Module-level KNOWN_PROVIDERS Set: anthropic, openai, openrouter, google, local, claude-code, opencode, z-ai, zen-mcp
budget-check.safe-cast: Uses KNOWN_PROVIDERS.has() to validate provider string before casting to Provider type -- avoids unsafe as-cast
budget-check.skip-on-exceeded: If limit.allowed is false, skip provider and log warning with percentUsed
budget-check.fail-closed: If checkLimit() throws, log warning and skip the provider. For unknown provider strings not in the Provider type, still attempt the check but catch type errors gracefully.

error.message: providerChain is empty -- configure at least one provider entry for this role.

error.context: Structured errors array with per-provider error details (sanitized)
error.retryable-rationale: The chain itself does not retry. Callers (engine) should retry the entire chain resolution with backoff if all providers are temporarily circuit-open.

upstream-dependencies.dependency: IProviderHealthTracker (interface from types.ts), ProviderHealthTracker (concrete class, for buildKey() static method), errors.ts (createProviderError, isProviderError)

downstream-consumers.consumer: RoleBasedAgentResolver -- depends on IProviderChain interface, creates ProviderChain per agent role
Engine integration -- uses resolver which uses IProviderChain

dependencies.upstream: Story 9-3: Provider Health Tracker (IProviderHealthTracker interface from types.ts, ProviderHealthTracker concrete for buildKey(), errors.ts)
dependencies.downstream: Story 9-8: Role-Based Agent Resolver (depends on IProviderChain interface)

acceptance-criteria.criteria: Returns first healthy provider from the ordered chain
Skips unhealthy (circuit-open) providers, returns the next healthy one
Throws NO_AVAILABLE_PROVIDER with provider names (not raw error messages) when all providers fail; detailed errors in structured context
Throws EMPTY_PROVIDER_CHAIN error (retryable: false, severity: 'critical') when entries array is empty
Skips providers that exceed budget (costTracker.checkLimit().allowed === false)
Returned provider is wrapped with InstrumentedAgentProvider (records usage)
Calls provider.dispose() when isAvailable() returns false
Calls provider.dispose() on error; dispose errors are logged, not silently swallowed
Unknown provider string does not crash budget check
Does NOT call recordSuccess() after isAvailable()
Uses isProviderError() type guard for recordFailure() calls
Constructor uses ProviderChainOptions object (not positional params)
Entries are defensively copied and frozen in constructor
Uses IProviderHealthTracker interface (not concrete ProviderHealthTracker class)
Uses IAgentProviderFactory interface (not concrete AgentProviderFactory class)
ProviderChain implements IProviderChain interface
Uses ProviderHealthTracker.buildKey() for centralized key construction
Budget check uses fail-closed policy: if checkLimit() throws, skip the provider
Module-level KNOWN_PROVIDERS constant (not re-created per iteration)
Inner provider is disposed if InstrumentedAgentProvider constructor throws

unit-tests.test: Returns first healthy provider from chain
All fail -- throws NO_AVAILABLE_PROVIDER with provider names (not raw error messages in message string)
NO_AVAILABLE_PROVIDER error has structured context.errors array with per-provider details
Empty chain -- throws EMPTY_PROVIDER_CHAIN error (retryable: false, severity: 'critical')
Budget check throws -- skips provider (fail-closed policy)
provider.dispose() called when isAvailable() returns false
provider.dispose() called on error; dispose errors are logged
Unknown provider string does not crash budget check
Does NOT call health.recordSuccess() after isAvailable()
Uses isProviderError() type guard -- ProviderError passed to recordFailure()
Uses isProviderError() type guard -- plain Error causes recordFailure() without error arg
Entries are defensively copied and frozen
InstrumentedAgentProvider constructor throw disposes inner provider

testing-requirements.testing-approach: All dependencies (factory, health, diagnostics, costTracker, logger) are mocked. Health mock implements IProviderHealthTracker interface. Factory mock implements IAgentProviderFactory interface. Tests verify interaction patterns and error handling without real provider instances.

risk.description: Resource leak if provider.dispose() throws during error cleanup
risk.mitigation: Dispose errors are caught and logged (not silently swallowed): this.logger?.warn('Failed to dispose provider', { key, error })

risk.description: Budget check may block valid providers if costTracker is misconfigured or throws
risk.mitigation: costTracker is optional; budget check uses fail-closed policy (if checkLimit() throws, skip provider and log warning); unknown provider strings skip budget check gracefully

risk.description: All providers may be circuit-open simultaneously during widespread outage
risk.mitigation: Circuit breaker has half-open recovery; NO_AVAILABLE_PROVIDER error includes all provider names for debugging. Callers (engine) should retry chain resolution with backoff.

risk.description: Resource leak if InstrumentedAgentProvider constructor throws
risk.mitigation: Inner provider is disposed in catch block when wrapping fails

task.title: Implement ProviderChain core iteration with health checks
task.scope: IProviderChain interface, ProviderChainOptions interface, class skeleton, constructor with defensive copy/freeze, getProvider() loop with IProviderHealthTracker.isHealthy() check using buildKey(), IAgentProviderFactory.create(), isAvailable() check, empty chain guard, NO_AVAILABLE_PROVIDER final throw with sanitized errors

task.title: Add budget checking via ICostTracker
task.scope: Module-level KNOWN_PROVIDERS constant, costTracker.checkLimit() integration with fail-closed try/catch, safe provider string validation, skip-on-exceeded logic

task.title: Add instrumentation wrapping and error handling
task.scope: InstrumentedAgentProvider wrapping (with dispose on constructor throw), isProviderError() type guard usage, recordFailure() calls, error collection array, logged dispose errors

task.title: Handle edge cases, disposal, and comprehensive tests
task.scope: provider.dispose() on isAvailable()===false, dispose on error with logging, empty chain test, unknown provider budget test, all-fail test, budget check throw test, defensive copy test, InstrumentedAgentProvider constructor throw test, full test suite
